description: wait_for_dma_complete uses a bounded for-loop with iteration cap and sentinel return; wait_for_buffer_swap and wait_for_overlay_ready use an unbounded while loop. The bounded loop is load-bearing on the DMA path — the external SPI bus is known to wedge under EMI on rev 1.4 boards, and an unbounded poll hangs the kernel.

findings.rationale: wait_for_dma_complete polls inside a bounded for-loop with explicit return on success and on timeout; the other two siblings spin in an unbounded while loop. Different LOOP shapes (for-with-counter vs while-with-condition), extra BRANCH for the timeout return.
